summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a logarithmic function?

A logarithmic function y = log_b(x) is the inverse of the exponential function y = bˣ, meaning it answers 'What exponent of b gives x?'

What is the vertical asymptote of a logarithmic graph?

The vertical asymptote marks the boundary of the domain; the graph gets infinitely close to this line but never reaches it.

What is a horizontal shift of a logarithmic function?

In y = log_b(x − h), the graph shifts right by h units (or left if h is negative), and the vertical asymptote moves to x = h.

What is a vertical shift of a logarithmic function?

In y = log_b(x) + k, every point on the graph moves up by k units (or down if k is negative), but the vertical asymptote does not change.

What is a logarithmic scale?

Logarithmic scales (like pH, Richter, and decibels) represent data spanning many orders of magnitude in a compact, readable form.

What is the x-intercept of y = log_b(x)?

Setting y = 0 gives log_b(x) = 0, so x = b⁰ = 1. The x-intercept is always (1, 0).

The pH of a solution is defined as pH = −log[H⁺]. If the hydrogen-ion concentration is [H⁺] = 0.001, what is the pH?

pH = −log(0.001) = −log(10⁻³) = −(−3) = 3.

A student graphs y = log₃(x − 2) + 1. What is the vertical asymptote of this function?

The argument (x − 2) must be greater than 0, so x > 2. The vertical asymptote is at x = 2; the vertical shift of +1 does not affect it.

An engineer models a signal strength as y = 2 log(x). What is the value of y when x = 1,000?

y = 2 log(1000) = 2 × 3 = 6, since log(1000) = 3.

A biologist knows that y = log(x) passes through (100, 2). If the graph is shifted up 3 units to y = log(x) + 3, what is the new y-value at x = 100?

The original value at x = 100 is log(100) = 2. Shifting up 3 gives 2 + 3 = 5.

A chemist's pH meter reads 5. Using pH = −log[H⁺], what is the hydrogen-ion concentration [H⁺]?

5 = −log[H⁺] means log[H⁺] = −5, so [H⁺] = 10⁻⁵ = 0.00001.

A data analyst transforms values using y = ln(x). If x = e³, what is y?

y = ln(e³) = 3, because ln and the exponential base e are inverse operations.

The graph of y = log₂(x) is reflected over the x-axis to create y = −log₂(x). What is the value of y when x = 8?

log₂(8) = 3 (since 2³ = 8), so y = −log₂(8) = −3.

A researcher models sound intensity with L = 10 log(I/I₀). If sound A has L₁ = 60 dB and sound B has L₂ = 40 dB, what is the ratio of their intensities I₁/I₂?

L₁ − L₂ = 10 log(I₁/I₂), so 60 − 40 = 10 log(I₁/I₂), giving log(I₁/I₂) = 2 and I₁/I₂ = 10² = 100.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A computer scientist uses f(x) = log₂(x) to measure information in bits. How many bits of information are in a message with 32 equally likely outcomes?

f(32) = log₂(32) = 5, since 2⁵ = 32. The message contains 5 bits of information.
